paper_title: Analysis of Radiative Feedbacks and IPSL-CM4 Coupled Climate Model Simulations

methods.description: A simple feedback analysis was performed to quantify the main drivers of changes in the top of the atmosphere radiative budget (TOA) at high latitudes (60-80°N). This analysis focuses on the local radiation fluxes within the atmospheric column, ignoring dynamical heat advection effects.

steps.description: Quantify the main drivers of changes in the TOA radiative budget at high latitudes.
steps.searchable_summary: perform feedback analysis on TOA radiative budget

steps.description: Display analyses of the TOA radiative budget terms and key feedbacks around Greenland.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize TOA radiative budget and feedbacks

steps.description: Characterize the radiative feedbacks involved in the linear response of the IPSL-CM4 simulated summer Greenland surface temperature with respect to summer insolation.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze radiative feedbacks for summer insolation response

steps.description: Compare the seasonal cycle and magnitude feedbacks at play in 126 ka and 2 × CO2 simulations.
steps.searchable_summary: compare seasonal cycle and feedbacks for 126 ka and 2x CO2

steps.description: Compare the seasonal cycle of atmospheric precipitable water anomaly as a function of surface temperature anomaly for 115, 126 ka and 2 × CO2 simulations.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze precipitable water anomaly vs temperature anomaly
outputs: comparison of precipitable water and temperature anomalies

methods.description: The IPSL-CM4 coupled climate model is used to simulate climate conditions under various orbital configurations and increased CO2 concentrations. The model couples atmospheric, ocean, and sea ice components to explore climate responses.

steps.description: Run IPSL-CM4 model simulations for various orbital configurations during the current and last interglacial periods.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate climate with IPSL-CM4 for different orbital configurations

steps.description: Run simulations under projected increased CO2 concentrations, including 2x and 4x CO2 scenarios.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate climate with increased CO2 using IPSL-CM4

steps.description: Average model outputs over the last 100 years of the simulations.
steps.searchable_summary: average simulation outputs over last 100 years

steps.description: Implement a parameterization of Greenland melt to explore feedbacks between Greenland warming, meltwater flux, and thermohaline circulation.
